page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance allocates 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core, establishing a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring proportional compute and memory scaling. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options for persistent data.

This hardware configuration is designed for enterprise applications, web serving, and distributed application nodes. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a balanced resource density, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that do not require specialized accelerators or massive memory-to-core ratios. The lack of bundled local storage and public IPv4 addresses means network and storage architecture must be provisioned separately, which allows for granular resource allocation and cost management.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio, providing exactly 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not feature bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, making it dependent on network-attached storage for data persistence and operating system boot volumes. It is designed for general-purpose workloads, including enterprise application hosting, web serving, and middle-tier database nodes, offering a balanced resource profile that avoids the premium costs associated with specialized compute or memory-optimized hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 192 GB of system memory, designed for balanced enterprise applications and web serving workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated x86_64 cores and one hundred ninety-two gigabytes of memory.

No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
